stories.intent: Per CIO directive 1 (Spool Session 6): flip eclipse-obd.service off simulate mode and harden service restart. Three bundled changes to deploy/eclipse-obd.service and /etc/systemd/journald.conf. Without this, the collector runs simulate forever (Pi's been generating 352K synthetic 'real' rows since Sprint 14) and we cannot capture real OBD. stories.title: BT-Resilient Collector -- reconnect loop + error classification + event types
stories.intent: Per Spool Session 6 drill: CIO's hypothesis confirmed that BT drop today kills the collector. The main capture loop must classify errors into ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE / ECU_SILENT / FATAL and stay alive across BT flaps. No systemd work (eclipse-obd.service exists; US-210 hardens its restart policy). Scope is the Python-side resilience: reconnect-wait loop with 1/5/30/60s backoff cap; new connection_log event_types for post-hoc drill analysis. Soft-blocker for US-208 real-drive validator to produce meaningful output.

Error classification function at the capture-loop boundary buckets raised exceptions into 3 classes. Fake exceptions in tests cover each class's dominant signatures.

On ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE: cleanly close python-obd connection (release self._connection), log bt_disconnect + adapter_wait to connection_log, enter ReconnectLoop; on successful probe, log reconnect_success, reopen OBD, resume.
On ECU_SILENT: stay connected; reduce poll cadence or pause (Ralph picks); log ecu_silent_wait; resume when ECU responds.
On FATAL: log with full traceback; re-raise (systemd handles via Restart=always from US-210).

feedback.notes: Implementation decisions: (1) event_type column stays TEXT with no CHECK constraint -- US-211 invariant 'ADDITIVE' takes precedence over the acceptance-criterion 'CHECK constraint accepts new values' because existing dynamic writers (profile switcher event.eventType, shutdown/command_core.py f'shutdown_{event}') emit runtime-composed strings that a restrictive CHECK would reject. (2) Existing ConnectionRecoveryMixin (state-change-driven, background thread) is NOT replaced -- new BtResilienceMixin coexists for the synchronous error-class-driven path. Over time the legacy path can be narrowed. (3) Classifier order critical: TimeoutError/ObdConnectionTimeoutError checked BEFORE OSError because TimeoutError is OSError subclass in Py3.10+; wrong order false-classifies every timeout as ADAPTER_UNREACHABLE. Dedicated test test_classifyCaptureError_timeoutWithRfcommSignature_isAdapter pins the boundary. (4) Integration test uses FakeObdConnection + scripted probe + FakeSleep for ~0 wall-clock deterministic runs; mixed with real ObdDatabase so connection_log rows actually land + are readable end-to-end. No wiring into data_logger.py in this sprint -- orchestrator exposes handleCaptureError(); data-logger integration follows in a later story when the capture-loop refactor happens (stop condition #2 avoided). stories.title: Benchtest data_source hygiene -- explicit tagging on non-live-OBD writers
stories.intent: Close the root cause of US-205's 352K surprise. US-195 DEFAULT 'real' was intended as a safety net for the single live-OBD writer, but it also tags every benchtest / simulator / dev writer that doesn't pass data_source explicitly. Result: filter WHERE data_source='real' stops meaning 'from the real car.' Post-US-210 the simulate service is off, so the pollution pressure drops sharply, but the bug itself stays -- any future benchtest run or hidden dev writer recreates the mess. This story audits every non-live-OBD INSERT into capture tables and adds explicit data_source tagging.

stories.title: TD-029 close -- deploy-time server migration gate (Alembic or explicit)
stories.intent: Close the CI gap that bit US-205: SQLAlchemy model changes in src/server/models/ do NOT propagate to live MariaDB because CI tests only against ephemeral SQLite. US-209 applied the missing migrations one-shot, but the underlying class-of-bug recurs any time someone adds a server-side column. This story wires a deploy-time migration gate so future Sprint-14-style schema additions auto-apply on deploy-server.sh. Two viable approaches: (1) adopt Alembic as canonical (`alembic upgrade head` runs at deploy time); (2) keep per-feature migration scripts but add an explicit 'run migrations' step in deploy-server.sh --init and default flow. Ralph picks based on codebase fit; TD-029 captures both as options.

Result: two rows per drive on server, downstream consumers must UNION-join. Per Ralph's inbox proposal Option 1: converge on one row per drive. Pi-sync creates the row with drive_id + source_device + metadata; analytics updates the same row (found via (source_device, drive_id)) with start_time/end_time/duration/row_count/is_real. Requires _ensureDriveSummary to know drive_id at analytics-run time -- which it does (server has received drive_id via sync since US-200).

stories.title: Power-Down Orchestrator -- staged 30/25/20 SOC shutdown + battery health log
stories.pmSignOff: Marcus 2026-04-21: L-size AFFIRMED by Spool audit (possibly L+). Gate LIFTED -- Spool audit landed at offices/pm/inbox/2026-04-21-from-spool-power-audit.md 2026-04-21. Audit found most of the ~1530-line power-mgmt codebase is DEAD (PowerMonitor, BatteryMonitor, power_db, power_display, readers, tiered_battery -- all never instantiated in production). Only UpsMonitor (MAX17048 polling) + ShutdownHandler (binary 10% trigger) run today. US-216 scope is NOT narrowed -- builds 30/25/20 state machine + 4 stage behaviors + hysteresis + config schema + regression tests fresh on top of UpsMonitor's polling. Reuses BatteryHealthRecorder (US-217 shipped). Critical TD-D inside scope: suppress ShutdownHandler legacy 30s timer + 10% trigger to prevent racing new ladder. US-216 writes to battery_health_log ONLY (power_log stays dead; separate follow-up TD deletes PowerMonitor after US-216 proves the ladder). Signed off for execution.
stories.intent: Per CIO directive 2 (Spool Session 6): implement staged shutdown at warning 30% / imminent 25% / trigger 20% SOC. Without this, UPS drain test showed Pi hard-crashes at zero. Spool audit (2026-04-21) confirms: existing ShutdownHandler has only binary 10%-low-battery trigger + 30s timer-after-BATTERY, NOT a staged ladder. US-216 builds the state machine fresh on top of UpsMonitor's MAX17048 polling, reuses UpsMonitor + BatteryHealthRecorder (US-217), wraps ShutdownHandler._executeShutdown() for the terminal action, and MUST suppress the legacy 30s timer + 10% trigger to prevent racing the new ladder. Four stage behaviors (warning flag DB + stop drive_id + sync push; imminent stop polling + close BT + force KEY_OFF; trigger battery_health_log close + poweroff; AC-restore cancel + drain-event-closed-as-recovered). Regression test mocks 100->0% drain and asserts ladder fires BEFORE 10% fallback.

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg --files-with-matches 'ShutdownHandler|UpsMonitor|onPowerSourceChange|getBatteryPercentage' src/ to map every call site. Cross-reference with Spool audit per-file dispositions. Record filesActuallyTouched.

New src/pi/power/orchestrator.py exposes PowerDownOrchestrator class with state machine NORMAL/WARNING/IMMINENT/TRIGGER. Hysteresis: +5% required above threshold to return to prior state (prevents 29<->31 oscillation).

WARNING@30%: sets DB flag pi_state.no_new_drives=true; BatteryHealthRecorder.startDrainEvent(startSoc, load_class='production') returns event_id (stashed for endDrainEvent at TRIGGER); triggers SyncClient force-push if network up.

IMMINENT@25%: stops poll-tier dispatch (no new Mode 01 queries); closes BT connection via US-211 clean-close API; on active drive, calls DriveDetector.forceKeyOff(reason='power_imminent') which closes drive + runs drive-end analytics hook.
TRIGGER@20%: BatteryHealthRecorder.endDrainEvent(event_id, endSoc) closes the drain row; ShutdownHandler._executeShutdown() called once to systemctl poweroff. Process exits.

AC-restore (UpsMonitor source-change callback BATTERY->EXTERNAL during any non-NORMAL state): cancel pending stages; BatteryHealthRecorder.endDrainEvent(event_id, endSoc=current, notes='recovered'); state returns to NORMAL.

ShutdownHandler legacy suppression: when config pi.power.shutdownThresholds.enabled=true (default), ShutdownHandler's 30s timer + 10% trigger DO NOT fire. When enabled=false (dev/test), legacy behavior preserved. Test asserts both paths.

REGRESSION TEST: mocked UpsMonitor walks SOC 100->0% over condensed interval; orchestrator fires all 3 stages (WARNING@30, IMMINENT@25, TRIGGER@20); _executeShutdown called exactly ONCE at 20%; legacy 10% trigger did NOT fire (would have been 2 poweroff calls). If this test doesn't exist, the story doesn't pass.

invariants: Hysteresis: once WARNING fires at <=30%, do not de-escalate to NORMAL until SOC >= 35% (warningSoc + hysteresisSoc). Same +5% rule at other thresholds.
TRIGGER (systemctl poweroff) is a one-way action. No recovery path from TRIGGER.
battery_health_log drain event MUST be closed (end_timestamp + end_soc + runtime_seconds) before poweroff; INSERT on WARNING, UPDATE on TRIGGER or AC-restore.
REUSE existing UpsMonitor + ShutdownHandler + BatteryHealthRecorder. Do NOT rewrite their internals.
Legacy ShutdownHandler 30s timer + 10% trigger MUST NOT fire when new ladder is enabled. The regression test_ladder_vs_legacy_race MUST prove this or story fails.
Do NOT revive PowerMonitor / BatteryMonitor / power_log. Those stay dead; separate TD (Sprint 17+) deletes them after US-216 proves the ladder.
Do NOT add new I2C reads -- UpsMonitor polls MAX17048 already at 5s cadence.
Do NOT call systemctl poweroff from anywhere except the TRIGGER stage action. Do NOT call from a test (mock subprocess.run).
Config thresholds are dev-overridable; production defaults (30, 25, 20, 5) stay unless CIO changes.
NEVER run to 0% on battery -- poweroff at 20% provides ~3-5 min safety margin per Spool's 23:49 baseline.
stopConditions: STOP if config.json pi.power.shutdownThresholds.enabled=true but pre-existing config has hardware.ups.lowBatteryThreshold pointing at 10% -- document interaction; the new ladder should win but verify hardware_manager's toggle correctly suppresses the legacy path.
STOP if hysteresis behavior on simulated drain fires stages multiple times (e.g., WARNING -> NORMAL -> WARNING on 29/31 oscillation) -- hysteresis gap; fix before closing.
STOP if the regression test (test_ladder_vs_legacy_race) cannot demonstrate new ladder fires BEFORE legacy 10% -- the race is real (per audit TD-D); diagnose + fix suppression path.

STOP if AC-restore cancellation during IMMINENT state leaves OBD polling stopped + BT closed + drive in KEY_OFF -- the cancellation path must fully restore operational state, not just abort the shutdown trigger.

STOP if SOC source-change callback chain broke during the 2026-04-20 drain (per audit's Hypothesis 2 'hardware_manager.start() raised an exception swallowed at lifecycle.py:476') -- this is a latent separate bug; file inbox note with lifecycle.py evidence, not scope creep into US-216.

feedback.completionNotes: Wired the post-drive review ritual without duplicating analysis/prompt logic. Four-step bash driver (post_drive_review.sh) orchestrates: (1) report.py --drive-id N, (2) new spool_prompt_invoke.py CLI that imports _buildAnalyticsContext + _loadSystemMessage + _renderUserMessage + _parseRecommendations from src/server/services/analysis.py (so CLI + auto-analyze paths render byte-identical prompts), (3) cat offices/tuner/drive-review-checklist.md (reused as-is -- no new checklist file), (4) printed pointer to offices/tuner/reviews/drive-N-review.md + offices/pm/inbox/<date>-from-spool-drive-N-review.md. Ollama config comes exclusively from config.json server.ai.{ollamaBaseUrl,model,apiTimeoutSeconds} via a minimal ${VAR:default} expander that matches secrets_loader semantics (no hardcoded endpoint per invariant). report.py got a single --drive-id alias added to the existing --drive arg via argparse's multiple-option-names feature (dest=drive), keeping report.py analysis logic untouched per doNotTouch. Graceful-exit contract per invariant: empty DB / missing tables / drive not found / empty drive window / Ollama unreachable or HTTP-erroring / empty JSON response all exit 0 so Steps 3+4 always emit (CIO can read the checklist + pointer even when Ollama is offline); exit 2 is reserved for argument parse errors only. SQLAlchemy OperationalError / SQLAlchemyError / AiAnalyzerGenerationError / OllamaUnreachableError / OllamaHttpError / FileNotFoundError / KeyError on config are all caught and print human-readable messages. **Filename deviation from filesToTouch**: story spec listed tests/scripts/test_post_drive_review.sh but I created tests/scripts/test_post_drive_review_sh.py (Python subprocess wrapper) instead, matching the more-recent test_replay_pi_fixture_sh.py precedent -- a pure .sh would not be auto-discovered by pytest and would require an additional .py wrapper anyway. Drift flagged per Session 71 drift-observation rule: minor and consistent with repo convention, no downstream impact. **Cross-component loadOllamaConfig fix**: initial implementation used `configPath: Path = _CONFIG_PATH` as a default argument, which Python evaluates at def-time and makes monkeypatch.setattr(module, '_CONFIG_PATH', new) ineffective. Fixed by reading the module attribute lazily inside the function body (`if configPath is None: configPath = _CONFIG_PATH`). Test coverage: 24 unit tests (test_spool_prompt_invoke.py) + 11 subprocess tests (test_post_drive_review_sh.py) -- all 35 pass cleanly off-Pi, off-Ollama, off-network. Verification: ruff check on all touched Python files = PASS; new tests 35/35 PASS. shellcheck not available on this host (documented in Sprint 14 baseline -- CIO-side machine) so --shellcheck step deferred to deploy-time. Sprint 16 status post-US-219: 9/10 stories passed; only US-216 remains (Spool-audit-gated, still waiting on offices/pm/inbox/<DATE>-from-spool-power-audit.md per sprintNotes GATE rule).
